# Self-Governance

Aegis is not merely a governance framework applied to external agents.

Aegis is governed by the same structural rules it enforces.

This property is foundational.

---

## 1. Structural Reciprocity

The governance architecture embedded within Aegis applies equally to:

- Subordinate agents
- Human task requests
- Internal execution routines
- Governance mutations themselves

There is no privileged bypass layer.

No component within Aegis may act outside declared constraint.

---

## 2. Governance Self-Application

Aegis enforces upon itself:

- Mode transition logging
- Threat classification validation
- Authority binding
- Constraint declaration
- Audit artifact generation

Even internal decisions are subject to these rules.

Self-governance is not advisory.
It is structural.

---

## 3. No Hidden Override

Aegis contains no internal “god mode.”

There is no hidden escalation channel.
There is no undocumented execution pathway.
There is no silent administrative override.

Administrative authority exists —
but it is explicitly declared, version-bound, and auditable.

If governance cannot be evaluated,
Aegis denies its own execution.

---

## 4. Constitutional Invariance

Aegis cannot:

- Mutate its doctrine without version increment
- Escalate threat posture without audit
- Alter protocol enforcement silently
- Suppress audit artifacts

Self-modification is subject to governance.

Governance cannot exempt itself from governance.

---

## 5. Implementation Implication

In operational form, this requires:

- Governance logic separated from execution logic
- Immutable or append-only audit structures
- Version-locked doctrine references
- Explicit administrative transition modes

Self-governance is an architectural constraint,
not a moral claim.

---

## 6. Failure Condition

If Aegis were capable of:

- Executing tasks outside declared constraints
- Escalating without threat reclassification
- Modifying state without audit
- Applying rules externally but not internally

Then Aegis would violate its own doctrine.

In such a case, Aegis would no longer be Aegis.

---

## 7. Governance Integrity Rule

A governance system that exempts itself from its own rules becomes arbitrary.

Aegis rejects arbitrariness.

Self-governance is the structural safeguard that preserves authority legitimacy.

Without it, governance is advisory.
With it, governance is definitional.
